paragraph: The condo and townhouse market is where the volume lives in Cliffside Park, with 74 sales over six months at a median of $557,000. This category has real breadth, ranging from $250,000 to $1,490,000, so you're looking at everything from entry-level units to substantial properties. The sale-to-list ratio sits at 0.99 with about 32 percent of homes selling above asking, which signals a competitive but balanced market where priced homes are moving without frenzied multiple offers. Median days on market of 28.5 days reflects steady interest without urgency. Single-family homes are sparse at 16 sales and a median of $842,000, with a ratio of 0.98 and only 19 percent above asking. These properties take longer to move, around 35 days, suggesting sellers need to price thoughtfully to generate traction. Multi-family properties are even more limited but move quickly, median $750,000 with 14 days on market and 40 percent selling above asking. Co-ops round out the market at the entry point with a median of $272,500 and 6 sales over the period. They carry board approval, financing restrictions, and resale friction that not every buyer is aware of going in. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo, or the lower price point of a co-op.
